title: Prevention of Hepatitis B Virus Infection in the United States: Recommendations of the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices | MMWR
text:

HepB vaccine coverage (≥3 doses) among children aged 19–35 months and 13–17 years is 90.5% ( 30 ) and 91.4% ( 33 ), respectively. Vaccine coverage (≥3 doses) is lower among adults: 27.4% among adults who report chronic liver conditions; 31.6% among adults who traveled outside the United States to countries other than Europe, Japan, Australia, New Zealand, or Canada since 1995; and 24.4% among adults with diabetes aged 19–59 years and 12.6% of adults with diabetes aged ≥60 years ( 34 ). Among health care personnel (HCP), ≥3-dose coverage was 64.7%, an increase from 51% in 1992 shortly after implementation of the Needlestick Safety and Prevention Act ( 35 ), but well below the Healthy People 2020 target of 90% ( 32 , 34 ).
New strategies for further reducing HBV transmission in this report include testing HBsAg-positive pregnant women for HBV DNA to identify infants at greatest risk for infection and guide the use of maternal antiviral therapy ( 36 , 37 ).
